_meta.purpose: Spec 35 Task 2 — the CLUSTER member sets (Text / Fill / Layout / Position / Motion) that make the element-manifest CLUSTER-COHERENCE rule computable. Expanded from three to six clusters per FR-35-2, then `flow` was merged back into `layout` the same day — see note_on_layout_merge. Five clusters.
_meta.source: Derived from setting-registry.json's css-property rows (golden master, 60 rows), grouped per the cluster table in .claude/plans/2026-07-20-spec-35-cluster-vocabulary-rework-design.md (FR-35-2). Every member.key below is a real setting_key in setting-registry.json — this file adds the missing CLUSTER axis + the default attr-suffix candidates the conformance checker uses to resolve a member to a block attribute (or a native `supports` path) when a block doesn't declare an explicit attrMap.
_meta.note_on_suffixes: `suffixes` are candidate PascalCase attribute-name suffixes tried in order against `{elementPrefix}{suffix}` when the element manifest has no explicit attrMap entry for that member. `nativeSupportsPath` (where present) is an ADDITIONAL resolution path: if the block's block.json `supports` object has a truthy value at that dot-path, the member counts as satisfied via WordPress-native supports (e.g. `spacing.padding`, `__experimentalBorder.radius`) even with no matching flat attribute — this is the real mechanism `sgs/brand-strip`'s wrapper-level padding/margin/border already uses (Spec 32 no-inline contract).
_meta.note_on_absorbs: `absorbs` (where present) lists OTHER setting-registry css rows that this member subsumes, so they need no member of their own. Used for the per-side box rows (css:padding-top/right/bottom/left, css:margin-*) which the shared BoxControl already covers via the merged `css:padding` / `css:margin` member. The FR-35-3 coverage validator counts an absorbed row as CLUSTERED — without this, the 8 per-side rows would be reported as false unclustered errors. Added 2026-07-20 (FR-35-2 merge case).
_meta.note_on_appliesToLayers: `appliesToLayers` (where present) scopes a member to elements at specific structural layers. It exists because ELEMENT is the PRIMARY mapping axis and cluster is SECONDARY: the 12 ARRANGEMENT members (display/flex-*/grid-*/justify-*/align-*/order/overflow) describe how an element arranges its CHILDREN, so they only apply to elements that HAVE children to arrange — layer OUTER or GRID. The 14 BOX members (padding/margin/gap/width/height/border-*/box-shadow/aspect-ratio) carry no appliesToLayers and apply to every element. NOTE `css:gap` is deliberately a BOX member, not arrangement: a leaf element legitimately has an internal gap (brand-strip's tile spaces its logo from its caption via logoGap). Tagging it as arrangement cost a real resolved member — caught and reverted 2026-07-20. Resolution rule: a member with appliesToLayers is checked ONLY when the element's `layer` is in that list, OR the element has no `layer` but is `isWrapper: true` (a wrapper arranges children by definition). Without this, merging flow into layout asks a leaf tile whether it has grid-template-columns — 144 false gaps measured on 2026-07-20 before this was added.
_meta.note_on_layout_merge: The `flow` cluster (grid/flex arrangement) was merged INTO `layout` on 2026-07-20, reversing an earlier same-day split. Rationale (Bean): arrangement properties do control the layout of the items in a block, and the ELEMENT axis — not a second cluster — is what disambiguates 'size this box' from 'arrange these children'. role='layout' on a GRID element means arrangement; the same role on a tile/box element means sizing. One mechanism, not two. Element is the PRIMARY mapping axis (block_attributes.canonical_slot); cluster/role is SECONDARY.

states._note: Spec 35 FR-35-5 — the STATES axis. A state is a COMPONENT concept (hover/focus/selected/pressed/disabled); its CSS realisation is declared ONCE here, centrally, so no block re-derives it. States are DECLARED on a manifest element (never parsed from attribute names — see FR-35-5 'Why states are DECLARED, never parsed from names': tabActiveTextColour renders as [aria-selected="true"], NOT CSS :active, and pauseOnHover/effectHover/imageZoomHover/grayscaleHover contain 'Hover' but are booleans/preset selectors, not state-variant style properties).

hover.label: Hover
hover.note: Also emit :focus-visible where the control is focusable (a11y parity).

focus.label: Focus
focus.note: NOT bare :focus — avoids the mouse-click focus ring.

pressed.label: Pressed
pressed.note: Genuine mouse-down. Rare; distinct from selected.

animation.label: Animation
animation.owningComponent: ToggleGroupControl (preset picker) + RangeControl (ms unit) + ToggleGroupControl (easing presets)
